title: SoD violations via break-glass usage — emergency role abuse
exclusions: Pairs with UC-22.40.2 for post-use approval.
evidence: Real-time 'breakglass_sod_violation' detects SoD violations during break-glass usage.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Break-glass that crosses SoD boundaries must be compensated; this UC generates the signal at session close.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a break-glass session performs actions that cross an SoD boundary (per sod_matrix.csv) without a pre-approved emergency-exception ticket, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Break-glass sessions that stay within the permitted action set do NOT fire.

spl:
index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h
| stats values(action_category) as actions by session_id account
| mvexpand actions
| lookup sod_matrix.csv action_a=actions OUTPUT toxic_against
| mvexpand toxic_against
| join session_id toxic_against [search index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h | eval hit=action_category]
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=emergency:exception | fields session_id exception_id]
| where isnull(exception_id)
| table session_id account actions toxic_against
description: Detects break-glass sessions that executed actions which would normally violate segregation of duties. Unless there is an emergency-exception ticket, the session breaches SoD even if the break-glass approval is otherwise valid.
value: Break-glass is often abused to step around SoD; this UC catches that pattern without waiting for audit.
implementation: (1) Tag PAM session actions into SoD categories; (2) maintain sod_matrix.csv; (3) require emergency exceptions to log to GRC; (4) run hourly.
visualization: Table of sessions with SoD crossings, single-value 'SoD-compliant emergency sessions'.
knownFalsePositives: Sessions whose category mapping is imprecise can look like crossings; refine sod_matrix.csv categories quarterly.
